senses.definition: plural of carder: a person who cardes; in modern usage, a thief who uses stolen credit card details or other card data to commit fraud, or someone who operates in the shadowy world of carding online.
examples: The police chased the carders who had stolen thousands in credit card data.
Online forums sometimes discuss techniques used by carders.

senses.definition: a person who owns or has the right to use a bank card (credit or debit) linked to an account.
examples: The cardholder must sign the back of the card.
Please enter your PIN, cardholder.

senses.definition: plural of cardholder; multiple people who hold or own cards.
examples: Cardholders are advised to review recent transactions.
The event offered special rates for cardholders.

senses.definition: a fictional or rare term; not widely used in contemporary English; may refer to a structure or concept made of cards, or be a proper noun/place name in some contexts.
examples: The old cardhouse stood on the pier as a curious sight.

senses.definition: rare or partial root form; may appear in medical terms as a stem related to heart (cardio-).
examples: In the term cardio-, the root 'cardi' refers to the heart.

senses.definition: the upper opening of the stomach or the esophageal opening into the stomach; medically refers to a condition of the heart region, or as in anatomy, cardiac entrance.
examples: Food passes through the cardia into the stomach.
Dysphagia can occur with diseases near the cardia.

senses.definition: relating to the heart.
examples: Cardiac arrest is a medical emergency.
Cardiac muscle pumps blood through the body.

senses.definition: relating to the heart; chiefly found in medical or poetic contexts; uncommon in everyday speech.
examples: Cardiacal symptoms appeared after the incident.

senses.definition: refers to a biological grouping within medical or anatomical contexts; often seen in historical or taxonomic lists.
examples: The class Cardiacea includes certain crustaceans.

senses.definition: a member of Cardiacea; a taxonomic term used in biology.
examples: Cardiaceans are studied in marine biology.

senses.definition: a term found in historical or anatomical texts, possibly referring to a small heart-like feature or a term used in taxonomy; not common in modern usage.
examples: The term cardiacle appears in older anatomical manuscripts.

senses.definition: informal plural for people who have heart-related conditions; can also refer to the heart as a group of people (the Cardiacs music band is a separate sense).
examples: The cardiacs in the ward were resting.
The Cardiacs are a rock band known for quirky music.

senses.definition: plural proper noun: Cardiacs, a British experimental rock band; use as a band name.
examples: The Cardiacs released a new album in the 1990s.

senses.definition: plural of cardia in anatomical terms; refers to the heart or cardiac opening in anatomy contexts.
examples: Anomalies at the cardiae can affect digestion.

senses.definition: rare or historical term; may refer to heart-related pain or a specific medical term; not common in modern usage.
examples: Ancient texts mention cardiagra as a form of chest discomfort.

senses.definition: a diagram illustrating the heart or related anatomy; a medical illustration.
examples: The cardiagram showed the flow of blood through the heart.

senses.definition: an instrument or device that records the action of the heart; related to electrocardiograph; not common in everyday language.
examples: The cardiagraph measures heart activity.

senses.definition: the science or technique of recording heart activity; field related to cardiography.
examples: Cardiography is essential in diagnosing heart conditions.

senses.definition: pertaining to the heart; used in medical contexts.
examples: Cardial tissue samples were examined.

senses.definition: pain in the heart area; chest pain.
examples: He experienced sudden cardialgia after exertion.
Doctors investigated the cause of the cardialgia.

senses.definition: plural of cardialgia; multiple instances of heart-area pain.
examples: The patient reported several episodes of cardialgias.

senses.definition: causing or relating to cardialgia; heart-area pain.
examples: Cardialgic symptoms prompted rapid testing.

senses.definition: plural of cardialgia; multiple pains in the heart area.
examples: He described several cardialgies over the week.

senses.definition: variant spelling of cardialgia; heart-area pain.
examples: The term cardialgy appears in older medical texts.

senses.definition: anatomical measure related to diameter of the heart or its parts; historical or rare term.
examples: Measurements included the cardiameter of the vessel.

senses.definition: rare or specialized term possibly referring to a heart-based morphological concept; not common in modern usage.
examples: Some early texts discuss cardiamorphia in cardiac development.
